figures.caption: Figure 2: Cognitive cycle. Every input passes through safety gates before and after inference.
Figure 4: Learning curve. P@4 improves with case base size; hard queries benefit most (+17.7%).
Table 3: Retrieval performance (N = 800, 200 queries, K = 4). Non-overlapping CIs for hybrid vs dense cosine.
Table 2: Specialist agents. Turn limits are defaults, configurable per profile.
Table 7: D′ decision sheet—debug email (comms agent, tighter thresholds: modify 0.30, reject 0.50). 0.51 > 0.50 → REJECT.
Table 6: D′ decision sheet—report delivery. 0.11 < 0.35 → ACCEPT.
Table 10: Affect input signals gathered from CognitiveState.
Figure 5: D′ scorer to normative calculus pipeline.
Table 9: BEAM/Gleam properties and their use in Springdrift.

Figure 6: OTP supervision tree. Red arrows show supervision relationships (parent restarts child on crash). Blue dashed arrows show typed message-passing channels. Each box is a BEAM process with its own heap and garbage collector.

Figure 1: System architecture. The cognitive loop orchestrates all components via typed message-passing channels. This is a simplified diagram; the system can also create teams of cooperating subagents.
